heading: Purpose of the Coast Guard Auxiliary
label: 3902 Purpose of the Coast Guard Auxiliary
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 14, U.S.C., 1946 ed., § 261 (, title I, § 2, ; , ).Feb. 19, 1941, ch. 855 Stat. 9Sept. 30, 1944, ch. 453, § 158 Stat. 759Changes were made in phraseology. 81st Congress, House Report No. 557.

Amendments2018—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–282, § 119(b)section 822 of this titleSubsec. (b)(2). , substituted “section 701” for “section 141”.Pub. L. 115–282, § 123(b)(2)2016— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), inserted heading, and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 114–1201996— inserted “of the Coast Guard Auxiliary” after “Purpose” in section catchline and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The purpose of the Auxiliary is to assist the Coast Guard:Pub. L. 104–324“(a) to promote safety and to effect rescues on and over the high seas and on navigable waters;“(b) to promote efficiency in the operation of motorboats and yachts;“(c) to foster a wider knowledge of, and better compliance with, the laws, rules, and regulations governing the operation of motorboats and yachts; and“(d) to facilitate other operations of the Coast Guard.”

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The purpose of the Auxiliary is to assist the Coast Guard as authorized by the Commandant, in performing any Coast Guard function, power, duty, role, mission, or operation authorized by law.
the Commandant has determined such waterway, or portion thereof, is navigable for purposes of the jurisdiction of the Coast Guard; or
a State or other proper authority has requested such patrol pursuant to or .section 701 of this titlesection 13109 of title 46
